Take the number you have left, square it, and then finally find the reciprocal of that squared number."}, {"index": 1, "question": "First, subtract 14 from 719. Using that clamped value, count how many in the list [14, 91, 90, 11, 49, 67, 48] are greater than it."}, {"index": 2, "question": "Count the items in the list [12, 79, 51, 79, 68, 94]. Multiply that count by 36, and then reduce the product by 25 percent."}, {"index": 3, "question": "Find the population standard deviation of [9, 31, 37, 62, 87, 37, 44]. Add 513 to that result. candidates.question: Start with 754 units and sell 33. Take the number you have left, square it, and then finally find the reciprocal of that squared number.
First, subtract 14 from 719. Then clamp the resulting number so it is between 77 and 158. Using that clamped value, count how many in the list [14, 91, 90, 11, 49, 67, 48] are greater than it.
Count the items in the list [12, 79, 51, 79, 68, 94]. Multiply that count by 36, and then reduce the product by 25 percent.
Find the population standard deviation of [9, 31, 37, 62, 87, 37, 44]. Add 513 to that result. Finally, determine how many numbers in [23, 94, 40, 1, 89, 31] are larger than that sum.
Calculate the population variance for [40, 53, 9, 5, 48, 16]. Subtract 56 from that variance. Then, count how many values in [94, 30, 91, 10, 2, 13, 84, 31] are less than the resulting number.
